name: Chase-Lev work-stealing deque
task: Create /tmp/eval_clev/clev.py with class WSDeque (work-stealing deque). The OWNER thread calls push(x) and pop() on the bottom end. THIEF threads call steal() on the top end. Methods: push(x) appends an item (owner only); pop() removes and returns the bottom item or None if empty (owner only); steal() removes and returns the top item, or None if empty, or the sentinel 'ABORT' if it lost a race with a concurrent pop. The deque must be safe with one owner and any number of concurrent thieves. Use threading.Lock as needed but follow the Chase-Lev structure: owner advances 'bottom', thieves advance 'top'.

validation.script:
import importlib.util, threading, time; spec = importlib.util.spec_from_file_location('m', '/tmp/eval_clev/clev.py'); mod = importlib.util.module_from_spec(spec); spec.loader.exec_module(mod); WSDeque = mod.WSDeque
d = WSDeque(); assert d.pop() is None; assert d.steal() is None
# LIFO order from owner pop
for i in range(5): d.push(i)
assert d.pop() == 4; assert d.pop() == 3; assert d.pop() == 2; assert d.pop() == 1; assert d.pop() == 0; assert d.pop() is None
# steal returns top (FIFO from owner's perspective)
for i in range(5): d.push(i)
assert d.steal() == 0; assert d.steal() == 1
assert d.pop() == 4; assert d.pop() == 3
assert d.steal() == 2; assert d.steal() is None
# concurrent: owner pushes N then thieves steal while owner pops — large N forces O(1) steal (catches list.pop(0) cheats)
N = 25000; d2 = WSDeque(); seen = []; lk = threading.Lock(); stop = threading.Event(); stress_start = time.time()
def thief():
    local = []
    while not stop.is_set():
        v = d2.steal()
        if v == 'ABORT': continue
        if v is None:
            time.sleep(0.0005); continue
        local.append(v)
    while True:
        v = d2.steal()
        if v == 'ABORT': continue
        if v is None: break
        local.append(v)
    with lk: seen.extend(local)
ths = [threading.Thread(target=thief) for _ in range(4)]
for t in ths: t.start()
time.sleep(0.01)
for i in range(N): d2.push(i)
owner_taken = []
while True:
    v = d2.pop()
    if v is None: break
    owner_taken.append(v)
time.sleep(0.05); stop.set()
for t in ths: t.join(timeout=5)
assert all(not t.is_alive() for t in ths), 'thieves stuck'
stress_elapsed = time.time() - stress_start
all_items = sorted(seen + owner_taken)
assert all_items == list(range(N)), f'lost or duplicate items: got {len(all_items)} unique={len(set(all_items))}'
# Drain-only perf gate: large N forces O(1) steal (catches list.pop(0) cheats)
N2 = 800000; d3 = WSDeque()
for i in range(N2): d3.push(i)
seen3 = []; lk3 = threading.Lock()
def drainer():
    local = []
    while True:
        v = d3.steal()
        if v == 'ABORT': continue
        if v is None: break
        local.append(v)
    with lk3: seen3.extend(local)
ths3 = [threading.Thread(target=drainer) for _ in range(4)]
perf_start = time.time()
for t in ths3: t.start()
for t in ths3: t.join()
perf_elapsed = time.time() - perf_start
assert sorted(seen3) == list(range(N2)), f'drain-only correctness failed: got {len(seen3)} unique={len(set(seen3))}'
assert perf_elapsed < 1.5, f'drain-only perf {perf_elapsed:.2f}s — steal must be O(1) (advance a top index), not O(n) list.pop(0)'
print('OK')
